hyperedges.label: Friday Three-Tier Memory System (Hot/Warm/Cold + Concept Graph)
Friday Integrated Stack (Frappe fork + Raven + ERPNext ported + Hermes-derived core)
Phase 1 Governed Framework Loop (profile + permission + sandbox + audit)

nodes.label: Phase 1.5: Hardening
nodes.rationale: Separate hardening phase allows Phase 1 to prove the governance loop first without over-engineering sandbox security; stronger defaults, egress allowlist/proxy, and warm container pool can be layered on once the core loop is validated.

nodes.label: Kubernetes Specialist Agent
nodes.rationale: Specialised agents with focused skill sets outperform generalists by 30-60% in token usage and 20-40% in task completion rate (per OpenClaw insight from doc 15).

nodes.label: Infrastructure Coordinator Agent
nodes.rationale: Coordinator never executes domain tasks directly to enforce separation of concerns; it routes to specialists and reconciles cross-cutting concerns.

nodes.label: Friday Decision Invariants
nodes.rationale: Captured as cross-cutting constraints from the full ideation session to prevent architectural drift across all future contributions.

nodes.label: pgvector Semantic Memory
nodes.rationale: No external vector DB chosen to reduce moving parts, lower cost, and simplify audit; pgvector inside PostgreSQL keeps the whole data layer in one system.

nodes.label: Skill DocType
nodes.rationale: Skills stored as structured DocTypes (queryable, permission-gated, status-flagged) rather than markdown files to provide governance and auditability that markdown files cannot.

nodes.label: Gateway-Level Permission Enforcement
nodes.rationale: Permission enforced at gateway before queueing rather than at runtime config level, making it structural rather than configurable and thus harder to bypass.

nodes.label: Security Claims Audit
nodes.rationale: Specific CVE numbers and vulnerability counts are reputationally expensive if wrong and legally adjacent; architectural-pattern language carries the same rhetorical force without fragile identifiers.

nodes.label: Framework Version
nodes.rationale: Local snapshots scoped to the project's exact framework version solve latency, hallucination risk, and version mismatch problems of web-search-as-you-go.

nodes.label: FridayLabs Hosted Platform
nodes.rationale: Open-core model: framework is GPL v3 free, convenience of hosting is paid; revenue flows back to contributors to make contributing economically rational, especially for Indian developers.

nodes.label: Phase 1 Governance Loop
nodes.rationale: Phase 1 proves governance (permission-first, audit trail) before breadth; the single governed loop end-to-end validates the architecture.

nodes.label: Eight Defense Layers
nodes.rationale: Security as architecture (not configuration): six independent layers between user input and host compromise, making permission misconfigurations caught structurally rather than silently ignored.

nodes.label: Frappe API Security Boundary
nodes.rationale: Containers cannot reach PostgreSQL/Redis directly; all reads and writes flow through Frappe REST API so even a fully compromised container cannot bypass the permission engine.

nodes.label: Frappe v16 Hard Fork
nodes.rationale: Hard fork chosen over app-on-top approach so agent-native primitives (actor context, trace, audit hooks, sandboxed execution) can be built into framework core; Frappe bench retained as excellent operational infrastructure.

nodes.label: Fork Discipline
nodes.rationale: Line between core modifications and app-level features keeps the core focused on framework vs domain concerns; every divergence documented in docs/core-divergences.md for future engineers.

nodes.label: Control Room Five Operator Questions
nodes.rationale: The trust contract between human supervisors and Friday agents requires these five questions answerable within two clicks; backend governance without a visible control surface is invisible governance.

nodes.label: Execution Replay
nodes.rationale: Replay is read-only reconstruction from logs rather than re-execution; operators must be able to see exactly what an agent did, not just a summary, to earn trust.

nodes.label: Skill Draft DocType
nodes.rationale: Agent never auto-promotes improvements to production; Skill Draft + supervisor approval flow prevents silent skill mutation, which is the enterprise governance failure mode auditors flag.

nodes.label: Skill Version Rollback
nodes.rationale: Rollback must be always safe because every version was previously validated; chained rollback prevents skill drift from being permanent.
